name: Billy Harper
abstract: Billy Harper (born January 17, 1943) is an American jazz saxophonist, "one of a generation of Coltrane-influenced tenor saxophonists" with a distinctively stern, hard-as-nails sound on his instrument.

concerts.label: New York Jazz Repertory Company: Seventy Five Years of Jazz
Kool Jazz Festival: The Blakey Legacy
Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: Saxophone Summit

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: I organized a group and we did some concerts. The group was Gary Bartz on alto saxophone, Billy Harper on tenor saxophone, Kenny Barron, Buster Williams, and Idris Mohammed on drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Noah Evans
about_quotes.evidence: Speaking specifically at this point about your trumpet player and your saxophone player, Billy Harper... Billy Harper who now plays with Gil

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Weston
about_quotes.evidence: I had Neil Clarke, Alex Blake; you had Billy Harper, T.K. Blue, and Benny Powell.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Weston — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part II
about_quotes.evidence: Benny Powell proposed a project we’re going to do soon, called the Trio of Griots... And Billy Harper, the tenor player. So I guess the three of us were the ones who were doing it most
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part II interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 18, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: David Weiss
about_quotes.evidence: Billy Harper with Max Roach. You know they came up through this stuff... I want the adventurous spirit of these guys. You know? To keep looking. That’s what the Cookers I think grabs.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Weiss interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Dodgion
about_quotes.evidence: one time we were doing a clinic with the Thad Jones saxophone section... I think Billy Harper says, 'Well I don’t copy anybody you know.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Dodgion interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996
